summary: Housekeeping PR that removed four deprecated Rust workspace tools: tools/config-dtk, tools/diff-symbols, tools/fix-labels, and tools/replace-module. The PR deleted their source files, Cargo.toml files, duplicated LICENSE files, config-dtk's standalone Cargo.lock and committed IntelliJ .idea metadata, then updated the root Cargo.toml workspace member list and Cargo.lock to drop the removed packages and now-unused dependencies.
what_changed: Removed tools/config-dtk from the root Rust workspace and deleted its Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, source files, LICENSE, and committed .idea project files.
Removed tools/diff-symbols from the root Rust workspace and deleted its Cargo.toml, src/main.rs, and LICENSE.
Removed tools/fix-labels from the root Rust workspace and deleted its Cargo.toml, src/main.rs, and LICENSE.
Removed tools/replace-module from the root Rust workspace and deleted its Cargo.toml, src/main.rs, and LICENSE.
Updated the root Cargo.lock to remove the deleted package entries melee-config-dtk, melee-diff-symbols, melee-fix-labels, and melee-replace-module.
Pruned now-unused Rust dependencies from Cargo.lock, including cwparse, dashmap, nom, nom-supreme, pathdiff, lock_api, parking_lot_core, and duplicate older Windows target package versions.
Normalized remaining Cargo.lock dependency references where duplicate versions were no longer needed, such as env_logger, itertools, bitflags, hashbrown, and windows-targets.
smart_moves: Removed the deprecated crates from Cargo.toml workspace members first, ensuring the root workspace no longer tries to build or resolve deleted tools.
Regenerated or updated the root Cargo.lock after deleting workspace members, which pruned transitive dependencies rather than leaving stale lockfile entries.
Deleted entire obsolete tool directories rather than leaving partial source or manifest fragments that could confuse future tooling work.
Included cleanup of committed IDE metadata under tools/config-dtk/.idea, reducing non-source repository noise alongside the deprecated tool removal.
decomp_lessons: When decomp-support tools become deprecated, remove them from the workspace manifest and lockfile together; otherwise Cargo may retain stale packages or workspace references.
Tooling cleanup can materially simplify the repository by dropping unused parser and label-manipulation dependencies, even when no game code changes.
After deleting Rust workspace crates, check for duplicate dependency versions in Cargo.lock because removing one crate can allow Cargo to collapse references to unqualified package names.
Avoid keeping editor-specific project files in tool subdirectories, especially when the tool itself is obsolete.
naming_conventions: Removed Rust package names used the melee-* prefix: melee-config-dtk, melee-diff-symbols, melee-fix-labels, and melee-replace-module.
Tool directories under tools/ used kebab-case names matching the package purpose: config-dtk, diff-symbols, fix-labels, replace-module.
Remaining workspace tools shown in Cargo.toml continue to live under tools/, including flags, issues, replace-symbols, and rust-utils.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available beyond the PR title and diff; conclusions are based on file deletions and workspace/lockfile updates.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that removed or replaced Rust tooling under tools/
Search for remaining references to config-dtk, diff-symbols, fix-labels, or replace-module after PR 1128
Find PRs that updated Cargo.toml workspace members and Cargo.lock together
Search for tooling migrations from deprecated Rust tools to replacement scripts or tools
